ea_prompt.parent_head_capture_note: Captured at SDO authoring time. EA must re-capture at the start of execution via `git rev-parse HEAD` on BlarAI (and confirm devplatform HEAD via `cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; git log --oneline -1`) and substitute live values into the comprehension comment. EA-3 is BlarAI-only writes; the devplatform HEAD is a sanity reference for EA-2-landed confirmation, not an amendment target.

attachment.reason: Authoritative scope; SS5.1 EA-3 deliverable specification; SS5.2 non-goals (no ADR/DEC amendments, no test-file edits, no production source); SS5.3 EA-3 boundary on whether to amend the SCR template symmetrically (EA's choice within boundary); SS7 sequencing (strictly after EA-2).

Sprint 11 continuation; lessons L-12 (verbatim comprehension recitation), L-13 (parent_head capture), L-15 (working-set declaration), L-22 (mature-not-minimal floors), L-25 (evidence-first: quote source documents verbatim, do not paraphrase).

Primary amendment target -- current state at lines 184-215 (SS5 Scope integrity analysis; SS5.4 Ghost commits already exists). Read end-to-end before editing to identify the precise insertion location (within SS5.4 as SS5.4.1, OR as new SS5.5, OR as expansion of SS5.4 with cross-repo applicability gate).

Secondary amendment target -- SS8.4 Parallel-Sprint Authorization at lines 124-175 currently references `docs/governance/parallel-sprints.md` (BlarAI-relative). That file moved to devplatform per Sprint 10 doctrine split; the relative path is now a broken pointer. Fix to absolute devplatform path.

Tertiary amendment target -- EVALUATE whether the SCR template has a SS5.4-equivalent ghost-commit subsection that warrants symmetric cross-repo amendment. SDV SS5.1 EA-3 says optional: amend if present, skip if absent. Read end-to-end to make the call; document the choice in the comprehension comment.

Sprint 10 SWAGR -- the manual cross-repo SS5.4 ghost-commit sweep at lines ~236-242 + the formal MINOR gap #6 row at line 641 that recommends template amendment. EA-3 codifies the manual style into the template. Confirm exact filename via Glob at EA start.

Sprint 9 SWAGR SS9.3(d) -- the original prediction (`SWAGR template lacks cross-repo section`) that Sprint 10 confirmed. EA-3 closes the loop on this prediction; cite both SWAGRs in the new template subsection's revision-log entry.

VERIFY THIS FILE DOES NOT EXIST IN BlarAI (it moved to devplatform per Sprint 10 EA-3). Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md should return False; Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md should return True. Confirms the broken-pointer fix surface.

The operational reference target for the SDV template SS8.4 pointer fix AND for the new SWAGR template cross-repo subsection. Read end-to-end so the new template subsection's prose grounds in what the doctrine actually says about cross-repo sweeps.

ea_prompt.pre_flight:
Sprint 11 EA-3 is the THIRD EA in Sprint 11. EA-1 (DEC bundle) and EA-2 (Active State refresh procedure) are landed on both BlarAI main and devplatform main. EA-3 is BlarAI-only writes: it amends three BlarAI template files (SWAGR + SDV + optional SCR), updates each amended template's revision log, and lands a Q1-1 ledger entry.
Working-set verification. Before any writes, Test-Path each of: - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_work_analysis_and_gap_report_template.md (must return True -- primary amendment target) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_design_vision_template.md (must return True -- secondary amendment target) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_completion_report_template.md (must return True -- tertiary, EA evaluates whether to amend) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\ (must return True -- ledger destination) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md (MUST return False -- confirms Sprint 10 doctrine split landed; the SDV template pointer is broken because the file no longer lives here) - C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md (MUST return True -- confirms target of the fixed absolute pointer)
Conflict check: no prior EA-3 staged or finalized prompt should exist in docs/scheduled/ea_queue/ outside the staging directory; no prior ledger entry with the EA-3 slug should exist: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI Test-Path docs\scheduled\ea_queue\*_ea3_*.xml Get-ChildItem docs\ledger\ -Filter '*sprint11_ea3*.md' Expected: False / empty. If conflicts, STOP and escalate -- do NOT overwrite.
Fleet-Pause SOP (per feedback_pause_fleet_for_git_work). PAUSE the fleet before any checkout or git work:
$env:PYTHONPATH = "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform"; python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.pause_fleet('Sprint 11 EA-3 SWAGR cross-repo template amendment', updated_by='ea_code', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')" cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git commit --allow-empty -m "chore(ops): pause fleet -- Sprint 11 EA-3 SWAGR cross-repo template"
Resume LAST, just before posting the completion comment: $env:PYTHONPATH = "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform"; python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.resume_fleet(updated_by='ea_code', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')" cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git commit --allow-empty -m "chore(ops): unpause fleet -- Sprint 11 EA-3 done"
API-NAME GOTCHA: function is `resume_fleet`, NOT `unpause_fleet`.
Serial execution context. EA-1 and EA-2 are landed -- no parallel sibling. EA-4 (test-baseline drift investigation) is gated on EA-3 merge per SDV SS7. Do NOT touch any EA-4 or EA-5 working-set path (see negative_constraints).
ea_prompt.comprehension_gate:
BEFORE any edits, checkouts, or branch creation, post a comprehension comment to Vikunja tracking task #410 prefixed `[agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension]` containing ALL of:
1. Milestone objective in your own words: what template amendments you are authoring; why now (Sprint 9 SWAGR SS9.3(d) prediction + Sprint 10 SWAGR gap #6 confirmation -- two-sprint signal to codify the cross-repo cross-repo ghost-commit sweep into the SWAGR template); who consumes the amendments downstream (the Sprint Auditor authoring the first post-EA-3 SWAGR -- Sprint 11's own SWAGR may exercise the new subsection, since Sprint 11 itself is cross-repo).
2. Per-deliverable work-item summary. WI-1 through WI-7 -- one sentence each in your own words.
3. Files to create/modify (absolute paths). EXACTLY between three and five files depending on SCR-symmetric-amendment choice: - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_work_analysis_and_gap_report_template.md (amend -- new cross-repo subsection + revision-log row) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_design_vision_template.md (amend -- SS8.4 broken pointer fix + revision-log row) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_completion_report_template.md (amend IFF EA-3 evaluates the SCR template has a SS5.4-equivalent ghost-commit subsection; otherwise SKIP this file -- document the skip-rationale in the comprehension comment and ledger entry) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\TS_sprint11_ea3_swagr-cross-repo-template.md (new; TS = UTC YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS captured at EA start)
NO other files are edited. No production source. No test code. No ADR. No DEC document. No CLAUDE.md. No active_tasks.yaml. No wake template. The SWAGR template insertion location is SS5.4.1 OR SS5.5 (EA-3 picks within SDV SS5.3 boundary by reading the existing SS5.4 "Ghost commits -- independent discovery" and choosing the structurally cleaner placement; document the choice in the comprehension comment).
4. Source files to read (read-only). All required_attachments above.
5. EXACT deliverable structure -- recite VERBATIM.
SWAGR template amendment (docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_work_analysis_and_gap_report_template.md): New subsection titled "Cross-repo ghost-commit sweep" with: (a) one-paragraph applicability gate (>= 3 sentences) stating "fill out this subsection only when the sprint writes to more than one repo's main branch during the sprint window; skip otherwise with a verbatim 'N/A -- single-repo sprint' note"; (b) populated table template with columns: `Repo | Commit window (HEAD-pre-sprint -> HEAD-post-sprint) | Sweep result | Substantive findings | Requires LA attention?` -- show one example row referencing the Sprint 10 sweep pattern; (c) prose guidance (>= 2 paragraphs) explaining the cross-repo classification (TRIVIAL / MINOR_UNDOC / SCOPE_DRIFT / INFRASTRUCTURE_FIX) and the cross-repo escalation surface -- some commits are out-of-sprint but cross-repo-unblocking (see Sprint 10 wake-template absolute-path fix pattern); (d) pointer line citing the operational reference: `Reference: C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md` (absolute path -- BlarAI -> devplatform cross-repo pointer style established by Sprint 10 EA-3). Length floor: >= 25 populated lines of new content (excluding insertion-context lines). Insertion location: EA-3 picks SS5.4.1 (within current SS5.4) OR SS5.5 (parallel to current SS5.4); the rationale is documented in the comprehension comment AND in the SWAGR template revision-log entry.
SDV template amendment (docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_design_vision_template.md): Section SS8.4 contains the broken pointer at line 138: `Reference: docs/governance/parallel-sprints.md for the full best-practice guide.` That relative path is BlarAI-relative; post Sprint 10 doctrine split, the file lives on devplatform. Replace with the absolute devplatform path: `Reference: C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md`. OPTIONAL (within SDV SS5.1 EA-3 scope): add a cross-repo row to the SS8.4.1 shared-artifact table reflecting that a sprint writing to both repos lists each repo's main as a shared artifact with `serialize-at-co-lead` mitigation. EA-3 picks within scope; document choice in the comprehension comment. Net new lines: >= 1 (pointer fix) + >= 0-5 (optional row).
SCR template amendment (docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_completion_report_template.md): EVALUATE: does the SCR template carry a SS5.4-equivalent ghost-commit subsection that would benefit from symmetric cross-repo amendment? If YES, amend symmetrically using the SWAGR template insertion pattern (>= 10 lines of new content; pointer to the operational reference; revision-log entry). If NO, SKIP this file and document the skip-rationale in the comprehension comment + ledger entry. The SDV explicitly permits skip per SS5.3 boundary.
Template revision-log entries: Each amended template's revision-log table (Appendix-style at end of file) gets a new row recording the v_prev -> v_new transition with date + change summary. Each row cites Sprint 9 SWAGR SS9.3(d) (original prediction) + Sprint 10 SWAGR gap #6 (confirmation) + Sprint 11 EA-3 (this work) as the motivation chain. Increment the template version (if a frontmatter version field exists; check at EA start).
Ledger entry (docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea3_swagr-cross-repo-template.md): Q1-1 schema per docs/ledger/README.md. Frontmatter (ledger_id, date, sprint_id=11, entry_type=EA, predecessor, branch= feature/p5-task11-ea3-swagr-cross-repo-template, merge_commit= null, disposition=COMPLETE) -> SSSummary -> SSDeliverables -> SSFiles Changed (BlarAI commit) -> SSQuality Gate -> SSDEC References (link to DEC-17 ledger-format if EA-1 has landed, which it has; link to DEC-15 sprint-lifecycle).
6. ORACLE expectation (verbatim, executed at EA close after the BlarAI commit lands):
cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git diff main...feature/p5-task11-ea3-swagr-cross-repo-template --name-only Expected (sorted, SCR-amended case -- 4 files): docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea3_swagr-cross-repo-template.md docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_completion_report_template.md docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_design_vision_template.md docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_work_analysis_and_gap_report_template.md
Expected (sorted, SCR-skipped case -- 3 files): docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea3_swagr-cross-repo-template.md docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_design_vision_template.md docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_work_analysis_and_gap_report_template.md
Any other path -> ORACLE violation; STOP and report.
7. Working-set declaration (L-15). Verbatim: "I write to exactly three or four absolute paths (three if I skip the optional SCR template amendment; four if I include it). All four are BlarAI-only. I read the devplatform parallel-sprints.md doctrine as a content source for the new SWAGR subsection but do NOT edit it -- devplatform writes are out-of-scope for EA-3 per SDV SS5.1. I touch no test file, no production source, no ADR, no existing DEC, no CLAUDE.md, no active_tasks.yaml, no wake template. If I discover a defect in a doctrine file or a contradiction between the existing template content and the Sprint 10 SWAGR manual sweep style, I STOP and escalate via the comprehension comment; I do NOT silently 'reconcile'."
8. Mature-not-minimal acknowledgment (L-22). Verbatim: "Mature not minimal: SWAGR new subsection >= 25 populated lines with a SUBSTANTIVE example row grounded in the Sprint 10 sweep pattern (not a `` row); ledger entry >= 35 lines content; template revision-log rows are descriptive (>= 1 sentence per row, citing motivation chain), not one-word entries. Padding to hit the floor is rejected -- a focused 28-line subsection with substantive content beats a 50-line subsection padded with redundant boilerplate."
9. Evidence-first discipline (L-25). Verbatim: "Every claim about what the existing templates say, what the Sprint 9/10 SWAGRs predicted/confirmed, or what the devplatform parallel-sprints.md doctrine prescribes is grounded in an empirical file read at the parent_head, with line-number citations in the comprehension comment. No paraphrasing of source text in the new template subsection -- direct quotes or close paraphrases with attribution."
10. No-cross-repo-write acknowledgment (Sprint-11-specific). Verbatim: "EA-3 is BlarAI-only writes. I do NOT touch any path under C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\ for editing. I read devplatform parallel-sprints.md as a content source for prose and pointer-target verification; I do not modify it. EA-2 amended the devplatform Co-Lead wake template -- EA-3 does not amend any devplatform file."
11. ADR / DEC non-amendment acknowledgment. Verbatim: "EA-3 does not author or amend any ADR or DEC document. The Sprint 11 DEC-16/17/18 bundle is EA-1's purview (landed). DEC-19 (cross- reference style decision record) is EA-5's optional purview. EA-3 ships template amendments only, with revision-log rows recording the change."
12. Risks and ambiguities. State any unresolved understanding before starting -- especially: (a) SWAGR insertion location (SS5.4.1 vs SS5.5) -- pick and explain; (b) SCR symmetric amendment ship-or-skip choice -- pick and explain; (c) optional SDV SS8.4.1 cross-repo row addition ship-or-skip -- pick and explain. Each choice is within SDV SS5.3 boundary; SDO does NOT pre-decide these.
After posting the comprehension comment, apply label `Gate:Pending-SDO` (id 9) on Vikunja task #410 and STOP. WAIT for SDO Phase 1a review verdict before doing any work. Do NOT bundle downstream work with the comprehension comment.

milestone.objective: Codify the cross-repo ghost-commit sweep pattern into the canonical BlarAI SWAGR template (new subsection within SS5 Scope integrity analysis), fix the SDV template SS8.4 broken pointer to parallel-sprints.md (which moved to devplatform in Sprint 10 EA-3), and optionally amend the SCR template symmetrically if it carries a ghost-commit-equivalent subsection. Update each amended template's revision log. Land one BlarAI feature-branch commit (merged via Co-Lead trusted_scope) plus Q1-1 ledger entry. Closes Sprint 9 SWAGR SS9.3(d) prediction and Sprint 10 SWAGR gap #6 confirmation.
milestone.size_rationale: Template-surgical edits across two-or-three BlarAI template files: new SWAGR subsection (~25-35 lines) + SDV pointer fix (~1-5 lines) + optional SCR symmetric amendment (~10-15 lines) + revision-log rows (~3-9 lines) + ledger entry (~35-50 lines). Aggregate ~75-115 lines, all docs.

work_items.wi:
WI-1: Pre-flight conflict + dependency check.
Verify input file presence via Test-Path: - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_work_analysis_and_gap_report_template.md -- must return True - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_design_vision_template.md -- must return True - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_completion_report_template.md -- must return True - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\ -- must return True - C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md -- must return True (the post-Sprint-10 destination)
Verify the SDV template's broken pointer surface: - Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md -- MUST return False (confirms Sprint 10 EA-3 doctrine split landed; the BlarAI-relative pointer in SDV template SS8.4 is the broken pointer to fix)
Verify no prior EA-3 artifact exists: - Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\scheduled\ea_queue\ -Filter '*_ea3_*.xml' -- expected: empty (or only the EA-3 staging file at docs\scheduled\ea_queue\staging\, which is THIS prompt) - Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\ -Filter '*sprint11_ea3*.md' -- expected: empty
Verify EA-1 + EA-2 commits landed on main: - cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git log --oneline main | Select-String 'sprint:11.*EA-1|Sprint 11 EA-1|sprint:11.*EA-2|Sprint 11 EA-2' | Select-Object -First 4 - cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; git log --oneline main | Select-String 'sprint:11' | Select-Object -First 4 - Expected: EA-1 + EA-2 merge/commit lines visible on BOTH repos.
Read each amended template end-to-end and identify the precise insertion locations: - SWAGR template: locate SS5.4 "Ghost commits -- independent discovery" (line ~206 at parent_head). Decide insertion location: SS5.4.1 (sub-subsection within current SS5.4) OR SS5.5 (new parallel subsection after SS5.4). Document the choice in the comprehension comment with the structural rationale. - SDV template: locate SS8.4 paragraph at line ~138 containing the broken pointer `docs/governance/parallel-sprints.md`. Capture verbatim the surrounding sentence for the commit-body before/after diff cite. - SCR template: scan the table of contents / section headers end-to-end. Determine whether a SS5.4-equivalent ghost-commit subsection exists. Document the YES/NO + ship-or-skip rationale in the comprehension comment.
Capture all Test-Path outputs + section-locating outputs verbatim for the ledger entry's SSQuality Gate section.

WI-2: Amend the SWAGR template. File: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_work_analysis_and_gap_report_template.md
Insert a new subsection (SS5.4.1 within current SS5.4 OR SS5.5 parallel to SS5.4 -- WI-1 chose) titled `Cross-repo ghost-commit sweep`.
Required content for the new subsection (recite verbatim in comprehension):
(a) Applicability gate paragraph (>= 3 sentences): - State that this subsection is filled out only when the sprint writes to more than one repo's main branch during the sprint window (e.g., a BlarAI + devplatform cross-repo sprint per the Sprint 10 / Sprint 11 pattern). - State the skip convention: write `N/A -- single-repo sprint` and proceed. - Cross-reference the SDV template SS8.4 Parallel-Sprint Authorization + Shared-Artifact Audit (companion authorization-side audit; this SWAGR subsection is the post-hoc verification side).
(b) Sweep table template: | Repo | Commit window (HEAD-pre-sprint -> HEAD-post-sprint) | Sweep result | Substantive findings | Requires LA attention? | |---|---|---|---|---| | `BlarAI` | `...` | `` | `` | `YES / NO` | | `devplatform` | `...` | `` | `` | `YES / NO` |
(c) Guidance prose (>= 2 paragraphs): - Paragraph 1: classification taxonomy. TRIVIAL = whitespace / typo. MINOR_UNDOC = small but undocumented scope addition. SCOPE_DRIFT = material undocumented addition warranting LA attention. INFRASTRUCTURE_FIX = cross-repo-unblocking commit attributed to LA/Co-Lead outside the sprint's EA chain (see Sprint 10 wake-template absolute-path fix sequence for the canonical pattern). - Paragraph 2: cross-repo escalation surface. Some commits are out-of-sprint but cross-repo-unblocking and must be attributed correctly -- they are not EA scope drift; they are LA/Co-Lead fleet-tooling fixes that unblock the cross-repo sprint. Mis-classifying such commits as SCOPE_DRIFT produces false-positive audit signal.
(d) Pointer line citing the operational reference: `Reference: C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md` (absolute path; BlarAI -> devplatform cross-repo pointer style as established by Sprint 10 EA-3.)
Length floor: >= 25 populated lines of new content (excluding adjacent insertion-context lines). Mature-not-minimal: the sample row in the sweep table is GROUNDED in the Sprint 10 SWAGR §5.4 manual-sweep pattern (real commit-window shape, real classification value) -- not `` text.
Add a SWAGR template revision-log row (Appendix at end-of-file) recording the change. The row format follows the existing log style; the row text cites Sprint 9 SWAGR §9.3(d) prediction + Sprint 10 SWAGR gap #6 confirmation + Sprint 11 EA-3 (this work) motivation chain.

WI-3: Amend the SDV template. File: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_design_vision_template.md
Two surgical edits within SS8.4 Parallel-Sprint Authorization & Shared-Artifact Audit:
(i) Pointer fix at line ~138 (verify exact line at WI-1): Replace the relative path `Reference: docs/governance/parallel-sprints.md for the full best-practice guide.` with the absolute devplatform path `Reference: C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md for the full best-practice guide.` (Adjust the exact surrounding prose only as needed for the substituted path; preserve sentence flow.)
(ii) OPTIONAL within SDV §5.1 EA-3 scope: add a cross-repo row to the SS8.4.1 shared-artifact table (lines ~140-150). Suggested row: | `devplatform main` (when cross-repo) | Y (via direct-to-main commit) | M (shared-mutable across cross-repo sprints) | Low (Co-Lead serializes; single direct-to-main commit per EA) | serialize-at-co-lead | EA-3 picks ship-or-skip within scope; document choice in comprehension comment + ledger entry.
Add an SDV template revision-log row (Appendix A at end-of-file) recording the change, with motivation-chain citation matching the SWAGR template row's structure.
If a frontmatter `sdv_version` or `template_version` field exists and is incrementable, bump it; otherwise leave unchanged.
Verification: independent re-grep at completion time -- Select-String -Path docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_design_vision_template.md -Pattern 'docs/governance/parallel-sprints.md' Expected: ZERO matches (the relative-path string no longer appears). The absolute path `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md` appears at least once. Capture both grep results verbatim in the ledger entry SSQuality Gate.

WI-4 (CONDITIONAL): Symmetric SCR template amendment. File: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_completion_report_template.md
Conditional on WI-1's evaluation: amend IFF the SCR template carries a §5.4-equivalent ghost-commit-discovery subsection or a sweep-style subsection that would benefit from symmetric cross-repo amendment.
If YES, amend symmetrically: append the same `Cross-repo ghost- commit sweep` content shape as WI-2 (>= 10 lines new content + pointer to operational reference + revision-log row). The SCR-side subsection emphasizes self-report disclosure (SCR is the sprint's own claim) vs SWAGR's independent verification posture.
If NO, SKIP this file entirely. Document the skip-rationale in: - the comprehension comment (under risks/ambiguities item (b)) - the ledger entry's §Summary (one sentence)
The SDV explicitly permits skip per §5.3 boundary.
If amended, the SCR template's revision-log gets the same motivation-chain citation row.

WI-5: Commit BlarAI side. Create feature branch and commit: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git checkout -b feature/p5-task11-ea3-swagr-cross-repo-template git add docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_work_analysis_and_gap_report_template.md git add docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_design_vision_template.md git add docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_completion_report_template.md (IF amended -- WI-4 YES) git add docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea3_swagr-cross-repo-template.md git commit -m "[sprint:11][role:ea_code][phase:completion] EA-3 SWAGR cross-repo template + SDV pointer fix + ledger"
Commit body cites: (a) the two-sprint motivation chain (Sprint 9 SWAGR §9.3(d) prediction + Sprint 10 SWAGR gap #6 confirmation); (b) the structural choice (§5.4.1 vs §5.5 insertion + SCR-amended-or-skipped); (c) the SDV §8.4 pointer-fix verbatim before/after (one line each); (d) one-sentence statement that EA-3 is BlarAI-only writes (no devplatform companion commit this EA).
DO NOT merge to BlarAI main yourself. Leave for Co-Lead trusted_scope merge at Phase 3.

WI-6: BlarAI ledger entry on the feature branch. Path: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\TS_sprint11_ea3_swagr-cross-repo-template.md where TS = UTC YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS captured at EA start.
Frontmatter (Q1-1 schema per docs/ledger/README.md, formalized by EA-1's DEC-17): --- ledger_id: TS_sprint11_ea3_swagr-cross-repo-template date: YYYY-MM-DD sprint_id: 11 entry_type: EA predecessor: branch: feature/p5-task11-ea3-swagr-cross-repo-template merge_commit: null disposition: COMPLETE ---
Body sections: §Summary (3-5 sentences): motivation chain (Sprint 9 prediction -> Sprint 10 confirmation -> Sprint 11 EA-3 codification); SWAGR insertion-location choice rationale; SCR ship-or-skip choice rationale; SDV §8.4.1 row ship-or-skip choice rationale. §Deliverables (bulleted, absolute paths): SWAGR template path + new-subsection line count; SDV template path + net-line change; SCR template path + net-line change (or "skipped"); this ledger entry. §Files Changed: single sub-table for the BlarAI commit hash + paths. §Quality Gate: verbatim WI-1 Test-Path outputs + WI-3 before/after grep outputs for the SDV pointer fix + line-count grep outputs for the SWAGR new subsection (e.g. `Select-String -Pattern '^### 5\.4\.1' OR '^### 5\.5'`). §DEC References: link to DEC-17 (Q1-1 ledger format -- landed in EA-1); link to DEC-15 (sprint-lifecycle, template ownership context).
Length floor: >= 35 lines content (excluding frontmatter).

WI-7: Post-commit ORACLE verification + completion comment composition. Execute:
cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git diff main...feature/p5-task11-ea3-swagr-cross-repo-template --name-only
Expected output is one of the two shapes documented in the comprehension gate item (6). Capture the verbatim output.
Additional Co-Lead-audit verification commands (capture verbatim output for the completion comment): (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_work_analysis_and_gap_report_template.md | Measure-Object -Line).Lines Select-String -Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_work_analysis_and_gap_report_template.md -Pattern 'Cross-repo ghost-commit sweep' Select-String -Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_design_vision_template.md -Pattern 'C:\\Users\\mrbla\\devplatform\\docs\\governance\\parallel-sprints.md' Select-String -Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\_templates\strategic_design_vision_template.md -Pattern 'docs/governance/parallel-sprints.md' # Expected: SWAGR new subsection title found >= 1 time; # SDV absolute-path pointer found >= 1 time; # SDV old relative-path pointer found ZERO times.
Verify no out-of-scope file appears in the diff. Any out-of-scope file -> ORACLE violation; STOP and report.
Do NOT push or merge. Leave for Co-Lead trusted_scope merge.

ea_prompt.negative_constraints:
DO NOT, under any circumstances:
1. Touch any path under C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\ for editing. EA-3 is BlarAI-only writes. devplatform parallel-sprints.md is READ-ONLY (content source for prose + pointer-target verification). EA-2 amended the devplatform Co-Lead wake template; EA-3 does not amend any devplatform file.
2. Author or amend any ADR or DEC document. The Sprint 11 DEC-16/17/18 bundle is EA-1's purview (landed). DEC-19 (cross- reference style decision record) is EA-5's optional purview. EA-3 ships template amendments only.
3. Touch any test file or production source. No edits to services/, shared/, launcher/, tests/, pyproject.toml, conftest.py.
4. Edit C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\CLAUDE.md, ACTIVE_SPRINT.md, docs/active_tasks.yaml, docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md, docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md (frozen at Entry 52 per DEC-17), or any ADR.
5. Touch any EA-4 or EA-5 working-set path. No edits to docs/sprints/sprint_11/test_baseline_drift_investigation.md (EA-4), .github/copilot-instructions.md (EA-5), tools/vikunja_mcp/README.md (EA-5), C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\scheduled\wake_templates\sprint_auditor.md (EA-5).
6. Touch any EA-1 or EA-2 working-set path. No edits to C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\decisions\DEC-16_*.md / DEC-17_*.md / DEC-18_*.md (EA-1); no edits to C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\runbooks\active_state_refresh.md (EA-2); no edits to C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\active_state_refresh.ps1 (EA-2); no edits to C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\scheduled\wake_templates\co_lead_architect.md (EA-2).
7. Touch any wake template on either repo. Sprint 11 wake-template work is EA-2 (Co-Lead) + EA-5 (Sprint Auditor) exclusive.
8. Invent new gate labels, lifecycle phases, or EA roles. EA-3 ratifies the existing Sprint 9 / Sprint 10 cross-repo sweep pattern; it does not invent.
9. Push to a remote main, force-push, or bypass Co-Lead trusted_scope merge. The branch lands via the standard feature-branch + Co-Lead merge flow.
10. Bundle downstream work with the comprehension comment. Post comprehension; apply Gate:Pending-SDO; STOP. Wait for SDO Phase 1a verdict before any edits.
11. Author the new SWAGR cross-repo subsection with placeholder content (``, ``, etc.). The mature-not-minimal floor requires a grounded example row built from the Sprint 10 manual-sweep pattern.
12. Silently reconcile inconsistencies between existing template content and the Sprint 10 SWAGR manual-sweep style. If a contradiction is found, STOP and escalate via the comprehension comment.
ea_prompt.mature_not_minimal: SWAGR new subsection >= 25 populated lines with a SUBSTANTIVE example row in the sweep table grounded in the Sprint 10 SWAGR §5.4 manual-sweep pattern (real classification value, real commit-window shape -- not placeholder text). Guidance prose spans >= 2 paragraphs with the cross-repo escalation surface explicitly called out (infrastructure-fix vs scope-drift classification distinction). SDV pointer fix preserves sentence flow; the optional shared-artifact-table row is shipped when it adds clarity vs noise (EA-3 judges). SCR amendment ships iff the SCR template carries a parallel ghost-commit subsection; skipped otherwise with a written rationale. Ledger entry >= 35 lines content with verbatim Quality Gate evidence (Test-Path + grep before/after). Padding to hit floors is rejected -- a focused 28-line SWAGR subsection with substantive content beats a 50-line subsection padded with redundant cross-references.
ea_prompt.cross_repo_ordering: EA-3 is BlarAI-only writes. There is no cross-repo commit ordering concern within EA-3 itself. EA-1 + EA-2 cross-repo commits are PRE-CONDITIONS that WI-1 verifies. The new SWAGR subsection REFERENCES devplatform parallel-sprints.md by absolute path; the devplatform side is read-only.
ea_prompt.oracle:
At EA close, after the BlarAI feature-branch commit lands:
cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git diff main...feature/p5-task11-ea3-swagr-cross-repo-template --name-only
Expected (sorted, SCR-amended case -- 4 files): docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea3_swagr-cross-repo-template.md docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_completion_report_template.md docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_design_vision_template.md docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_work_analysis_and_gap_report_template.md
Expected (sorted, SCR-skipped case -- 3 files): docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea3_swagr-cross-repo-template.md docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_design_vision_template.md docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_work_analysis_and_gap_report_template.md
Any other path = ORACLE violation. EA captures the diff verbatim in the completion comment on Vikunja task #410.
Additional verification commands for Co-Lead audit: Select-String -Path docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_work_analysis_and_gap_report_template.md -Pattern 'Cross-repo ghost-commit sweep' # Expected: >= 1 match (the new subsection heading) Select-String -Path docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_design_vision_template.md -Pattern 'C:\\Users\\mrbla\\devplatform\\docs\\governance\\parallel-sprints.md' # Expected: >= 1 match (the fixed absolute path) Select-String -Path docs/sprints/_templates/strategic_design_vision_template.md -Pattern 'docs/governance/parallel-sprints.md' -SimpleMatch # Expected: ZERO matches without the C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform prefix # (the old broken pointer string no longer appears)
ea_prompt.completion:
After ORACLE passes, post `[agent:ea_code][phase:completion]` comment on Vikunja tracking task #410 containing: 1. The BlarAI feature-branch commit hash. If Co-Lead trusted_scope merge has already landed, include the merge commit hash too. 2. The git diff --name-only output verbatim. 3. Line counts: SWAGR new subsection, SDV pointer-fix net change, SCR amendment (or "skipped"), ledger entry. 4. The TS timestamp used in the ledger filename. 5. Three EA-3 within-scope choices + one-sentence rationale each: (a) SWAGR insertion location (§5.4.1 vs §5.5); (b) SCR symmetric amendment (ship vs skip); (c) SDV §8.4.1 cross-repo row (ship vs skip). 6. Confirmation that all WI-1 Test-Path checks returned the expected shape (3 templates True, ledger dir True, devplatform parallel-sprints.md True, BlarAI governance dir False). 7. Verbatim WI-7 grep outputs verifying the SWAGR subsection title appears, the SDV absolute-path pointer appears, and the SDV old relative-path pointer does NOT appear. 8. Confirmation that no negative constraint was violated -- in particular: NO devplatform writes, NO ADR/DEC writes, NO test/production source writes, NO touch of EA-1/2/4/5 working-set paths.
Apply label Gate:Pending-SDO (id 9) on task #410. STOP. WAIT for SDO Phase 1b completion-review verdict.
Resume the fleet LAST BEFORE posting the completion comment -- ensures the fleet is unpaused for SDO's review-wake to fire cleanly.
ea_prompt.budget_envelope: Effective session cap: 60 min (+ active Budget Lift if granted). TTG: 2 h wall-clock. Trusted_scope merge eligibility: yes (Sprint 11 EA-3 BlarAI-side working set is 3-4 doc files, aggregate ~75-115 LOC; well under DEC-18 thresholds at 3000 LOC / 100 files; all paths under docs/sprints/_templates/ + docs/ledger/, which are doctrine surfaces inside the trusted_scope envelope).
ea_prompt.links: - Continuation: docs/P5_TASK11_SDO_CONTINUATION_v1.0.xml - Sprint 11 SDV (v3): docs/sprints/sprint_11/strategic_design_vision.md - DEC-13 report format: docs/DEC13_REPORT_QUEUE_PROPOSAL_v1.xml - Sprint 9 SWAGR §9.3(d): original prediction - Sprint 10 SWAGR §5.4 + gap #6 (line ~641): confirmation + formal MINOR recommendation - Sprint 10 EA-3 (BlarAI commit `cf95e4b` -- doctrine split move of parallel-sprints.md from BlarAI to devplatform; this EA fixes the resulting broken SDV-template pointer) - EA-1 DEC-17 (Q1-1 ledger format permanence -- this EA's ledger entry follows that schema)
